modules.title: Introduction of Signals and Systems
topics: Classification of signals and representation (continuous‑time, discrete‑time, even/odd, periodic/aperiodic, random/deterministic, energy/power, one/multidimensional, standard signals)
Basic operations on signals for continuous‑time / discrete‑time
Transformation of independent and dependent variables
Definition of system and its classification (CT/DT, linear/non‑linear, time‑variant/time‑invariant, causal/non‑causal, state/dynamic system)
System properties: linearity (additivity, homogeneity), shift‑invariance, causality, stability, realizability

modules.title: Linear Time‑Invariant Systems
topics: Impulse response representation for LTI systems
Properties of the impulse‑response representation for LTI systems
Block diagram representations (direct form‑I, direct form‑II, transpose, cascade, parallel)
Impulse response of DT‑LTI system and its properties

modules.title: z‑Transform
topics: Region of convergence (ROC) of finite‑duration sequences
Relation between discrete‑time Fourier transform and z‑transform
Analysis of discrete‑time LTI systems using the z‑transform

modules.title: Fourier Analysis of Discrete‑Time Signals
topics: Introduction to Fourier analysis of discrete‑time signals
Properties and applications of discrete‑time Fourier series
Convergence of the discrete‑time Fourier transform

modules.title: State‑Space Analysis and Multi‑Input Multi‑Output Representation
topics: State‑space analysis and multi‑input, multi‑output representation
The sampling theorem and its implications

experiments: Introduction to MATLAB Tool.
To implement delta function, unit step function, ramp function and parabolic function for continuous‑time.
To implement delta function, unit step function, ramp function and parabolic function for discrete‑time.
To implement rectangular function, triangular function, sinc function and signum function for continuous‑time.
To implement rectangular function, triangular function, sinc function and signum function for discrete‑time.
To explore the communication of even and odd symmetries in a signal with algebraic operations.
To explore the effect of transformation of signal parameters (amplitude‑scaling, time scaling & shifting).
To explore the time variance and time invariance property of a given system.
To explore causality and non‑causality property of a system.
To demonstrate the convolution of two continuous‑time signals.
To demonstrate the correlation of two continuous‑time signals.
To demonstrate the convolution of two discrete‑time signals.
To demonstrate the correlation of two discrete‑time signals.
To determine magnitude and phase response of Fourier Transform of given signals.
reference_books: Simon Haykin, “Signals and Systems”, John Wiley.
Simon Haykin, “Analog and Digital Communications”, John Willey.
Bruce Carlson, “Signals and Systems”, TMH.
Oppenheim & Wilsky, “Signals & Systems”, PHI.
Taub and Schilling "Principles of communication signals", 2nd ed. New York: Mcgraw Hill, 1986.

modules.title: Frequency domain representation of signal
topics: Condition of existence of Fourier transform

modules.title: Overview of communication system and amplitude modulation
Noise in analog communication
topics: Effect of noise on AM receivers
Effect of noise on FM receivers

experiments: To analyze characteristics of AM modulator & Demodulators.
To analyze characteristics of FM modulators & Demodulators.
To analyze characteristics of super heterodyne receivers.
To analyze characteristics of FM receivers.
To construct and verify pre‑emphasis and de‑emphasis and plot the waveforms.
To analyze characteristics of Automatic Volume Control and Automatic Frequency Control.
To construct frequency multiplier circuit and to observe the waveform.
To design and analyze characteristics of FM modulator and AM Demodulator using PLL.
reference_books: Simon Haykins, Communication System, John Willy
Singh & Sapre, Communication System, TMH
B.P. Lathi, Modern Digital and analog communication system; TMH
Singhal, analog and Digital communication, TMH
Rao, Analog communication, TMH
P K Ghose, principal of communication of analog and digital, universities press.
Taub & shilling, Communication System, TMH
Hsu; Analog and digital communication (Schaum); TMH
Proakis fundamental of communication system. (Pearson edition).

modules.title: Introduction to Control system
topics: Terminology and classification of control system
Mathematical modeling of mechanical and electrical systems
Feedback characteristics of control systems: open loop and closed loop systems
Effect of feedback on control system and on external disturbances

modules.title: Time response analysis
topics: Effects of addition of poles and zeros to open loop and closed loop system
Concept of stability of linear systems
Effects of location of poles on stability

modules.title: Frequency response analysis
topics: Correlation between time and frequency response
Assessment of relative stability using Nyquist plot and Bode plot (phase margin, gain margin and stability)

modules.title: Approaches to system design
topics: Design of phase lead-lag compensator in time and frequency domain
Proportional, derivative, integral and composite controllers

modules.title: State space representation of systems
topics: Relationship between state equation and transfer function

reference_books: Albert D. Helfrick, William David Cooper, “Modern electronic instrumentation and measurement techniques”, TMH 2008.
Oliver Cage, “Electronic Measurements and Instrumentation”, TMH, 2009.
Alan S. Morris, “Measurement and Instrumentation Principles”, Elsevier (Buterworth Heinmann), 2008.
David A. Bell, “Electronic Instrumentation and Measurements”, 2nd Ed., PHI, New Delhi 2008.
H.S. Kalsi, “Electronics Instrumentation”, TMH Ed. 2004
A.K.Sawhney, “A Course in Electrical and Electronic Measurements and Instrumentation”, Dhanpat Rai.
MMS Anand, “Electronic Instruments & Instrumentation Technology”, PHI Pvt. Ltd., New Delhi Ed. 2005

modules.title: Feedback Amplifier and Oscillators
topics: Concept of feedback and its types
Amplifier with negative feedback and its advantages

modules.title: Oscillators
topics: LC oscillators: Hartley, Colpitts, Clapp, and crystal oscillator

modules.title: Introduction to Integrated Circuits
topics: Advantages and characteristic parameters of ICs

modules.title: Operational Amplifier
topics: Characteristics and equivalent circuits of an ideal op‑amp

modules.title: Characteristics of Op‑amp
topics: Power‑supply rejection ratio (PSRR) and gain‑bandwidth product

modules.title: Op‑amp Applications
topics: Differential input and differential output amplifier
Magnitude and frequency response of filters

modules.title: Voltage Regulator
topics: Characteristics of standard regulator ICs: linear regulator, switching regulator, low‑dropout regulator

experiments: Measure and compare the op‑amp characteristics: offset voltages, bias currents, CMRR, slew rate of OPAMP LM741 and TL082.
Determine voltage gain and frequency response of inverting and non‑inverting amplifiers using TL082.
Design an instrumentation amplifier and determine its voltage gain using TL082.
Design op‑amp integrator (low‑pass filter) and determine its frequency response.
Design op‑amp differentiator (high‑pass filter) and determine its frequency response.
Design 2nd‑order Butterworth filter using universal active filter topology with LM741.
Design astable, monostable and bistable multivibrator using 555 and analyse its characteristics.
Implement automatic gain control (AGC) and automatic volume control (AVC) using multiplier MPY634.
Design a PLL using op‑amp with MPY634 and determine the free‑running frequency, capture range, and lock‑in range.
Design and test a low‑dropout regulator using op‑amps for a given voltage regulation characteristic and compare with TPS7250 IC.
reference_books: Ramakant A. Gaikwad, “OP‑Amp and Linear Integrated Circuits”, Third edition 2006, Pearson.
B. Visvesvara Rao, Linear Integrated Circuits, Pearson.

Maheshwary and Anand: Analog Electronics, PHI.

Sedra and Smith: Microelectronics, Oxford Press.

topics: Realization of network theorems using simulation
